page.content: In examining the treatment modalities utilized within the analyzed patient cohort, the data presented in the table elucidates significant trends in non-operative management approaches. Specifically, the non-operative treatment modality accounted for 96 patients, representing 22.7% of the entire cohort of 25,629 individuals. This subset illustrates a notable demographic composition, with a gender distribution of 76 females and 20 males, highlighting a potential area for further investigation into gender-specific responses to treatment.

page.content: Additionally, the mean age of patients receiving non-operative treatment was recorded at 70.5 years, with a range spanning from 43 to 102 years. The fracture types within this group were categorized, emphasizing the diversity of injuries managed non-operatively. The baseline and 12-week metrics of the cohort are essential for understanding the efficacy of treatments and tracking progress over time, particularly regarding the changes in *TCC levels, which are critical for assessing patient outcomes. This data underscores the importance of tailored treatment strategies in managing complex fracture cases in older populations.
